content.full_text:
May 11, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This office represents the Los Angeles County Federation of Labor, AFL-CIO (hereinafter "Federation"). On behalf of our client we are seeking advice pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) concerning the campaign contribution limits of the Political Reform Act.

The Federation is the county-wide parent organization made up of over 400 affiliated unions. Collectively, the Federation represents more than 700,000 individual union members working in Los Angeles County. The Federation sponsors a broad based political action committee registered with the Secretary of State. Many of the Federation's member unions also sponsor political action committees.

The Federation's sponsored PAC is planning a mailing and phone bank at the behest of a candidate within Los Angeles County for the June 1990 primary. The communications by the Federation will advocate the election of the candidate and will be directed only to members of the Federation. The total costs of the communications will exceed $5,000 this fiscal year.

To assist in paying the costs of the communications the Federation's sponsored PAC intends to request funds from its affiliated member union's sponsored PACs. Some of those transfers to the Federation's PAC may exceed $2,500 this fiscal year. The funds transferred would come from accounts maintained by the affiliated member's PACs into which only contributions of $2,500 or less per fiscal year have been deposited.

May 11, 1990
Page Two

The Federation is aware of the injunction in the SEIU v. FPPC lawsuit. The Federation requests advice in view of the injunction concerning the following question:

May the Federation's PAC receive transfers from its affiliated member's PACs in excess of $2,500 per fiscal year for the purpose of paying for communications made at the behest of a candidate in excess of $5,000 which are directed to the Federations and affiliated unions' individual members?

In view of the immediacy of the June primary election the Federation would request expedited advice regarding the above matter.

Thank you for your cooperation and assistance.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO:kh
